CheckTitle: Ensure app registrations use certificates instead of client secrets
Description: Identifies app registrations that have client secrets (passwords) instead of certificates or federated credentials. Certificates provide stronger authentication and cannot be easily exfiltrated from logs or config files.
Risk: Client secrets are strings that can leak through logs, source code, environment variables, or config files. Certificates are bound to the machine and provide non-repudiation. Microsoft recommends certificates over secrets for production applications.

Remediation.Recommendation: Replace client secrets with certificate credentials or workload identity federation. Remove existing client secrets after migrating to certificates.
